title: Use the variable in a panel

blocks.content:
Now that the variable exists, you need to reference it in a panel so the query targets whichever data source instance the user selects from the dropdown.

Instead of selecting a specific data source like "Prometheus-us-east", you select the variable. Grafana resolves it at query time based on the user's dropdown selection.

To use the variable in a panel, complete the following steps:

From the dashboard in edit mode, click **+ Add visualization** to create a new panel.

The panel editor opens.

In the **Data source** dropdown, search for the variable name you created. Data source variables appear with a `$` prefix (for example, `$datasource`).

Select the variable.

Write a query appropriate for the data source type. For TestData, leave the default scenario or select **Random Walk**.

The panel visualization updates using whichever data source instance is currently selected in the dashboard dropdown.
Click **Back to dashboard** to save the panel.

Try switching the data source dropdown at the top of the dashboard. The panel re-queries using the selected instance without any changes to the panel configuration.

In the next milestone, you enable multi-value selection so users can query multiple instances at once.
